fields.name: iap_getIapSettings
fields.description: Gets the IAP settings on a particular IAP protected resource.

fields.name: iap_projects_iap_tunnel_locations_destGroups_list
fields.description: Lists the existing TunnelDestGroups. To group across all locations, use a `-` as the location ID. For example: `/v1/projects/123/iap_tunnel/locations/-/destGroups`

args.name: pageSize
args.description: The maximum number of groups to return. The service might return fewer than this value. If unspecified, at most 100 groups are returned. The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 are coerced to 1000.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: A page token, received from a previous `ListTunnelDestGroups` call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page. When paginating, all other parameters provided to `ListTunnelDestGroups` must match the call that provided the page token.

fields.name: iap_projects_iap_tunnel_locations_destGroups_get
fields.description: Retrieves an existing TunnelDestGroup.

fields.name: iap_projects_brands_list
fields.description: Lists the existing brands for the project.

fields.name: iap_projects_brands_get
fields.description: Retrieves the OAuth brand of the project.

fields.name: iap_projects_brands_identityAwareProxyClients_list
fields.description: Lists the existing clients for the brand.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: The maximum number of clients to return. The service may return fewer than this value. If unspecified, at most 100 clients will be returned. The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced to 1000.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: A page token, received from a previous `ListIdentityAwareProxyClients` call. Provide this to retrieve the subsequent page. When paginating, all other parameters provided to `ListIdentityAwareProxyClients` must match the call that provided the page token.

fields.name: iap_projects_brands_identityAwareProxyClients_get
fields.description: Retrieves an Identity Aware Proxy (IAP) OAuth client. Requires that the client is owned by IAP.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: IapSettings
types.description: The IAP configurable settings.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Required. The resource name of the IAP protected resource.

types.name: AccessSettings
types.description: Access related settings for IAP protected apps.

fields.name: identitySources
fields.description: Optional. Identity sources that IAP can use to authenticate the end user. Only one identity source can be configured.

types.name: GcipSettings
types.description: Allows customers to configure tenant IDs for a Cloud Identity Platform (GCIP) instance for each application.

fields.name: tenantIds
fields.description: Optional. GCIP tenant IDs that are linked to the IAP resource. `tenant_ids` could be a string beginning with a number character to indicate authenticating with GCIP tenant flow, or in the format of `_` to indicate authenticating with GCIP agent flow. If agent flow is used, `tenant_ids` should only contain one single element, while for tenant flow, `tenant_ids` can contain multiple elements.

fields.name: loginPageUri
fields.description: Login page URI associated with the GCIP tenants. Typically, all resources within the same project share the same login page, though it could be overridden at the sub resource level.

types.name: CorsSettings
types.description: Allows customers to configure HTTP request paths that'll allow HTTP `OPTIONS` call to bypass authentication and authorization.

fields.name: allowHttpOptions
fields.description: Configuration to allow HTTP `OPTIONS` calls to skip authentication and authorization. If undefined, IAP will not apply any special logic to `OPTIONS` requests.

types.name: OAuthSettings
types.description: Configuration for OAuth login&consent flow behavior as well as for OAuth Credentials.

fields.name: loginHint
fields.description: Domain hint to send as hd=? parameter in OAuth request flow. Enables redirect to primary IDP by skipping Google's login screen. https://developers.google.com/identity/protocols/OpenIDConnect#hd-param Note: IAP does not verify that the id token's hd claim matches this value since access behavior is managed by IAM policies.

fields.name: clientId
fields.description: Optional. OAuth 2.0 client ID used in the OAuth flow. This allows for client sharing. The risks of client sharing are outlined here: https://cloud.google.com/iap/docs/sharing-oauth-clients#risks.

fields.name: clientSecret
fields.description: Optional. Input only. OAuth secret paired with client ID.

fields.name: clientSecretSha256
fields.description: Output only. OAuth secret SHA256 paired with client ID.

fields.name: programmaticClients
fields.description: Optional. List of client ids allowed to use IAP programmatically.

types.name: PolicyDelegationSettings
types.description: PolicyDelegationConfig allows google-internal teams to use IAP for apps hosted in a tenant project. Using these settings, the app can delegate permission check to happen against the linked customer project. This is only ever supposed to be used by google internal teams, hence the restriction on the proto.

fields.name: iamServiceName
fields.description: The DNS name of the service (e.g. "resourcemanager.googleapis.com"). This should be the domain name part of the full resource names (see https://aip.dev/122#full-resource-names), which is usually the same as IamServiceSpec.service of the service where the resource type is defined.

fields.name: iamPermission
fields.description: Permission to check in IAM.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The **relative** name of the resource, which is the URI path of the resource without the leading "/". See https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/conditions-resource-attributes#resource-name for examples used by other GCP Services. This field is **required** for services integrated with resource-attribute-based IAM conditions and/or CustomOrgPolicy. This field requires special handling for parents-only permissions such as `create` and `list`. See the document linked below for further details. See go/iam-conditions-sig-g3#populate-resource-attributes for specific details on populating this field.

fields.name: type
fields.description: The public resource type name of the resource. It is configured using the official_name of the ResourceType as defined in service configurations under //configs/cloud/resourcetypes. For example, the official_name for GCP projects is set as 'cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com/Project' according to //configs/cloud/resourcetypes/google/cloud/resourcemanager/prod.yaml This field is **required** for services integrated with resource-attribute-based IAM conditions and/or CustomOrgPolicy. This field requires special handling for parents-only permissions such as `create` and `list`. See the document linked below for further details. See go/iam-conditions-sig-g3#populate-resource-attributes for specific details on populating this field.

fields.name: service
fields.description: The name of the service this resource belongs to. It is configured using the official_service_name of the Service as defined in service configurations under //configs/cloud/resourcetypes. For example, the official_service_name of cloud resource manager service is set as 'cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com' according to //configs/cloud/resourcetypes/google/cloud/resourcemanager/prod.yaml This field is **required** for services integrated with resource-attribute-based IAM conditions and/or CustomOrgPolicy. This field requires special handling for parents-only permissions such as `create` and `list`. See the document linked below for further details. See go/iam-conditions-sig-g3#populate-resource-attributes for specific details on populating this field.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: The service defined labels of the resource on which the conditions will be evaluated. The semantics - including the key names - are vague to IAM. If the effective condition has a reference to a `resource.labels[foo]` construct, IAM consults with this map to retrieve the values associated with `foo` key for Conditions evaluation. If the provided key is not found in the labels map, the condition would evaluate to false. This field is in limited use. If your intended use case is not expected to express resource.labels attribute in IAM Conditions, leave this field empty. Before planning on using this attribute please: * Read go/iam-conditions-labels-comm and ensure your service can meet the data availability and management requirements. * Talk to iam-conditions-eng@ about your use case.

fields.name: expectedNextState
fields.description: The proto or JSON formatted expected next state of the resource, wrapped in a google.protobuf.Any proto, against which the policy rules are evaluated. Services not integrated with custom org policy can omit this field. Services integrated with custom org policy must populate this field for all requests where the API call changes the state of the resource. Custom org policy backend uses these attributes to enforce custom org policies. For create operations, GCP service is expected to pass resource from customer request as is. For update/patch operations, GCP service is expected to compute the next state with the patch provided by the user. See go/federated-custom-org-policy-integration-guide for additional details.

fields.name: locations
fields.description: The locations of the resource. This field is used to determine whether the request is compliant with Trust Boundaries. Usage: - Must not be empty for services in-scope for Trust Boundaries. Once Trust Boundaries is GA, empty values will cause the request to be rejected if customers enforce Trust Boundaries on the parent CRM nodes. - For global resources: use a single value of "global". - For regional/multi-regional resources: use name of the GCP region(s) where the resource exists (e.g., ["us-east1", "us-west1"]). For multi-regional resources specify the name of each GCP region in the resource's multi-region. NOTE: Only GCP cloud region names are supported - go/cloud-region-names.

types.name: JSON
types.description: The `JSON` scalar type represents JSON values as specified by [ECMA-404](http://www.ecma-international.org/publications/files/ECMA-ST/ECMA-404.pdf).

types.name: NextStateOfTags
types.description: Used for calculating the next state of tags on the resource being passed for the CheckCustomConstraints RPC call. The detail evaluation of each field is described in go/op-create-update-time-tags and go/tags-in-orgpolicy-requests.

fields.name: tags
fields.description: If TagsFullState is initialized, the values in this field fully represent all the tags in the next state (the current tag values are not used). If tags.size() == 0, the next state of tags would be no tags for evaluation purposes. Only one type of tags reference (numeric or namespace) is required to be passed.

fields.name: tagsToUpsert
fields.description: Tags that’ll be updated or added to the current state of tags for evaluation purposes. If a key exists in both "tags_to_upsert" and "tag_keys_to_remove", the one in "tag_keys_to_remove" is ignored. Only one type of tags reference (numeric or namespace) is required to be passed.

fields.name: tagKeysToRemove
fields.description: Keys of the tags that should be removed for evaluation purposes. IMPORTANT: Currently only numeric references are supported. Once support for namespace references is added, both the tag references (numeric and namespace) will be removed.

fields.name: tags
fields.description: If TagsFullStateForChildResource is initialized, the values in this field represent all the tags in the next state for the child resource. Only one type of tags reference (numeric or namespace) is required to be passed. IMPORTANT: This field should only be used when the target resource IAM policy name is UNKNOWN and the resource's parent IAM policy name is being passed in the request.

types.name: PolicyName
types.description: An internal name for an IAM policy, based on the resource to which the policy applies. Not to be confused with a resource's external full resource name. For more information on this distinction, see go/iam-full-resource-names.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Resource type. Types are defined in IAM's .service files. Valid values for type might be 'storage_buckets', 'compute_instances', 'resourcemanager_customers', 'billing_accounts', etc.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Identifies an instance of the type. ID format varies by type. The ID format is defined in the IAM .service file that defines the type, either in path_mapping or in a comment.

fields.name: region
fields.description: For Cloud IAM: The location of the Policy. Must be empty or "global" for Policies owned by global IAM. Must name a region from prodspec/cloud-iam-cloudspec for Regional IAM Policies, see go/iam-faq#where-is-iam-currently-deployed. For Local IAM: This field should be set to "local".

types.name: ReauthSettings
types.description: Configuration for IAP reauthentication policies.

fields.name: maxAge
fields.description: Optional. Reauth session lifetime, how long before a user has to reauthenticate again.

types.name: mutation_iap_updateIapSettings_accessSettings_reauthSettings_method
types.description: Optional. Reauth method requested.

types.name: mutation_iap_updateIapSettings_accessSettings_reauthSettings_policyType
types.description: Optional. How IAP determines the effective policy in cases of hierarchical policies. Policies are merged from higher in the hierarchy to lower in the hierarchy.

types.name: AllowedDomainsSettings
types.description: Configuration for IAP allowed domains. Lets you to restrict access to an app and allow access to only the domains that you list.

fields.name: enable
fields.description: Optional. Configuration for customers to opt in for the feature.

fields.name: domains
fields.description: Optional. List of trusted domains.

types.name: WorkforceIdentitySettings
types.description: WorkforceIdentitySettings allows customers to configure workforce pools and OAuth 2.0 settings to gate their applications using a third-party IdP with access control.

fields.name: workforcePools
fields.description: The workforce pool resources. Only one workforce pool is accepted.

types.name: OAuth2
types.description: The OAuth 2.0 Settings

fields.name: clientId
fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 client ID registered in the workforce identity federation OAuth 2.0 Server.

fields.name: clientSecret
fields.description: Input only. The OAuth 2.0 client secret created while registering the client ID.

fields.name: clientSecretSha256
fields.description: Output only. SHA256 hash value for the client secret. This field is returned by IAP when the settings are retrieved.

types.name: ApplicationSettings
types.description: Wrapper over application specific settings for IAP.

fields.name: cookieDomain
fields.description: The Domain value to set for cookies generated by IAP. This value is not validated by the API, but will be ignored at runtime if invalid.

types.name: CsmSettings
types.description: Configuration for RCToken generated for service mesh workloads protected by IAP. RCToken are IAP generated JWTs that can be verified at the application. The RCToken is primarily used for service mesh deployments, and can be scoped to a single mesh by configuring the audience field accordingly.

fields.name: rctokenAud
fields.description: Audience claim set in the generated RCToken. This value is not validated by IAP.

types.name: AccessDeniedPageSettings
types.description: Custom content configuration for access denied page. IAP allows customers to define a custom URI to use as the error page when access is denied to users. If IAP prevents access to this page, the default IAP error page will be displayed instead.

fields.name: accessDeniedPageUri
fields.description: The URI to be redirected to when access is denied.

fields.name: generateTroubleshootingUri
fields.description: Whether to generate a troubleshooting URL on access denied events to this application.

fields.name: remediationTokenGenerationEnabled
fields.description: Whether to generate remediation token on access denied events to this application.

types.name: AttributePropagationSettings
types.description: Configuration for propagating attributes to applications protected by IAP.

fields.name: expression
fields.description: Optional. Raw string CEL expression. Must return a list of attributes. A maximum of 45 attributes can be selected. Expressions can select different attribute types from `attributes`: `attributes.saml_attributes`, `attributes.iap_attributes`. The following functions are supported: - filter `.filter(, )`: Returns a subset of `` where `` is true for every item. - in ` in `: Returns true if `` contains ``. - selectByName `.selectByName()`: Returns the attribute in `` with the given `` name, otherwise returns empty. - emitAs `.emitAs()`: Sets the `` name field to the given `` for propagation in selected output credentials. - strict `.strict()`: Ignores the `x-goog-iap-attr-` prefix for the provided `` when propagating with the `HEADER` output credential, such as request headers. - append `.append()` OR `.append()`: Appends the provided `` or `` to the end of ``. Example expression: `attributes.saml_attributes.filter(x, x.name in ['test']).append(attributes.iap_attributes.selectByName('exact').emitAs('custom').strict())`

fields.name: outputCredentials
fields.description: Optional. Which output credentials attributes selected by the CEL expression should be propagated in. All attributes will be fully duplicated in each selected output credential.

fields.name: enable
fields.description: Optional. Whether the provided attribute propagation settings should be evaluated on user requests. If set to true, attributes returned from the expression will be propagated in the set output credentials.

types.name: ListTunnelDestGroupsResponse
types.description: The response from ListTunnelDestGroups.

fields.name: tunnelDestGroups
fields.description: TunnelDestGroup existing in the project.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: A token that you can send as `page_token` to retrieve the next page. If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

types.name: TunnelDestGroup
types.description: A TunnelDestGroup.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Identifier. Identifier for the TunnelDestGroup. Must be unique within the project and contain only lower case letters (a-z) and dashes (-).

fields.name: cidrs
fields.description: Optional. Unordered list. List of CIDRs that this group applies to.

fields.name: fqdns
fields.description: Optional. Unordered list. List of FQDNs that this group applies to.

types.name: ListBrandsResponse
types.description: Response message for ListBrands.

fields.name: brands
fields.description: Brands existing in the project.

types.name: Brand
types.description: OAuth brand data. NOTE: Only contains a portion of the data that describes a brand.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. Identifier of the brand. NOTE: GCP project number achieves the same brand identification purpose as only one brand per project can be created.

fields.name: supportEmail
fields.description: Support email displayed on the OAuth consent screen.

fields.name: applicationTitle
fields.description: Application name displayed on OAuth consent screen.

fields.name: orgInternalOnly
fields.description: Output only. Whether the brand is only intended for usage inside the G Suite organization only.

types.name: ListIdentityAwareProxyClientsResponse
types.description: Response message for ListIdentityAwareProxyClients.

fields.name: identityAwareProxyClients
fields.description: Clients existing in the brand.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: A token, which can be send as `page_token` to retrieve the next page. If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages.

types.name: IdentityAwareProxyClient
types.description: Contains the data that describes an Identity Aware Proxy owned client.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. Unique identifier of the OAuth client.

fields.name: secret
fields.description: Output only. Client secret of the OAuth client.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Human-friendly name given to the OAuth client.

fields.name: iap_setIamPolicy
fields.description: Sets the access control policy for an Identity-Aware Proxy protected resource. Replaces any existing policy. More information about managing access via IAP can be found at: https://cloud.google.com/iap/docs/managing-access#managing_access_via_the_api

fields.name: iap_getIamPolicy
fields.description: Gets the access control policy for an Identity-Aware Proxy protected resource. More information about managing access via IAP can be found at: https://cloud.google.com/iap/docs/managing-access#managing_access_via_the_api

fields.name: iap_testIamPermissions
fields.description: Returns permissions that a caller has on the Identity-Aware Proxy protected resource. More information about managing access via IAP can be found at: https://cloud.google.com/iap/docs/managing-access#managing_access_via_the_api

fields.name: iap_updateIapSettings
fields.description: Updates the IAP settings on a particular IAP protected resource. It replaces all fields unless the `update_mask` is set.

args.name: updateMask
args.description: The field mask specifying which IAP settings should be updated. If omitted, then all of the settings are updated. See https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/reference/google.protobuf#fieldmask. Note: All IAP reauth settings must always be set together, using the field mask: `iapSettings.accessSettings.reauthSettings`.

fields.name: iap_validateAttributeExpression
fields.description: Validates that a given CEL expression conforms to IAP restrictions.

args.name: expression
args.description: Required. User input string expression. Should be of the form `attributes.saml_attributes.filter(attribute, attribute.name in ['{attribute_name}', '{attribute_name}'])`

fields.name: iap_projects_iap_tunnel_locations_destGroups_create
fields.description: Creates a new TunnelDestGroup.

args.name: tunnelDestGroupId
args.description: Required. The ID to use for the TunnelDestGroup, which becomes the final component of the resource name. This value must be 4-63 characters, and valid characters are `[a-z]-`.

fields.name: iap_projects_iap_tunnel_locations_destGroups_delete
fields.description: Deletes a TunnelDestGroup.

fields.name: iap_projects_iap_tunnel_locations_destGroups_patch
fields.description: Updates a TunnelDestGroup.

args.name: updateMask
args.description: A field mask that specifies which IAP settings to update. If omitted, then all of the settings are updated. See https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/reference/google.protobuf#fieldmask

fields.name: iap_projects_brands_create
fields.description: Constructs a new OAuth brand for the project if one does not exist. The created brand is "internal only", meaning that OAuth clients created under it only accept requests from users who belong to the same Google Workspace organization as the project. The brand is created in an un-reviewed status. NOTE: The "internal only" status can be manually changed in the Google Cloud Console. Requires that a brand does not already exist for the project, and that the specified support email is owned by the caller.

fields.name: iap_projects_brands_identityAwareProxyClients_create
fields.description: Creates an Identity Aware Proxy (IAP) OAuth client. The client is owned by IAP. Requires that the brand for the project exists and that it is set for internal-only use.

fields.name: iap_projects_brands_identityAwareProxyClients_delete
fields.description: Deletes an Identity Aware Proxy (IAP) OAuth client. Useful for removing obsolete clients, managing the number of clients in a given project, and cleaning up after tests. Requires that the client is owned by IAP.

fields.name: iap_projects_brands_identityAwareProxyClients_resetSecret
fields.description: Resets an Identity Aware Proxy (IAP) OAuth client secret. Useful if the secret was compromised. Requires that the client is owned by IAP.

types.name: Policy
types.description: An Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy, which specifies access controls for Google Cloud resources. A `Policy` is a collection of `bindings`. A `binding` binds one or more `members`, or principals, to a single `role`. Principals can be user accounts, service accounts, Google groups, and domains (such as G Suite). A `role` is a named list of permissions; each `role` can be an IAM predefined role or a user-created custom role. For some types of Google Cloud resources, a `binding` can also specify a `condition`, which is a logical expression that allows access to a resource only if the expression evaluates to `true`. A condition can add constraints based on attributes of the request, the resource, or both. To learn which resources support conditions in their IAM policies, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/conditions/resource-policies). **JSON example:** ``` { "bindings": [ { "role": "roles/resourcemanager.organizationAdmin", "members": [ "user:mike@example.com", "group:admins@example.com", "domain:google.com", "serviceAccount:my-project-id@appspot.gserviceaccount.com" ] }, { "role": "roles/resourcemanager.organizationViewer", "members": [ "user:eve@example.com" ], "condition": { "title": "expirable access", "description": "Does not grant access after Sep 2020", "expression": "request.time < timestamp('2020-10-01T00:00:00.000Z')", } } ], "etag": "BwWWja0YfJA=", "version": 3 } ``` **YAML example:** ``` bindings: - members: - user:mike@example.com - group:admins@example.com - domain:google.com - serviceAccount:my-project-id@appspot.gserviceaccount.com role: roles/resourcemanager.organizationAdmin - members: - user:eve@example.com role: roles/resourcemanager.organizationViewer condition: title: expirable access description: Does not grant access after Sep 2020 expression: request.time < timestamp('2020-10-01T00:00:00.000Z') etag: BwWWja0YfJA= version: 3 ``` For a description of IAM and its features, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/).

fields.name: version
fields.description: Specifies the format of the policy. Valid values are `0`, `1`, and `3`. Requests that specify an invalid value are rejected. Any operation that affects conditional role bindings must specify version `3`. This requirement applies to the following operations: * Getting a policy that includes a conditional role binding * Adding a conditional role binding to a policy * Changing a conditional role binding in a policy * Removing any role binding, with or without a condition, from a policy that includes conditions **Important:** If you use IAM Conditions, you must include the `etag` field whenever you call `setIamPolicy`. If you omit this field, then IAM allows you to overwrite a version `3` policy with a version `1` policy, and all of the conditions in the version `3` policy are lost. If a policy does not include any conditions, operations on that policy may specify any valid version or leave the field unset. To learn which resources support conditions in their IAM policies, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/conditions/resource-policies).

fields.name: bindings
fields.description: Associates a list of `members`, or principals, with a `role`. Optionally, may specify a `condition` that determines how and when the `bindings` are applied. Each of the `bindings` must contain at least one principal. The `bindings` in a `Policy` can refer to up to 1,500 principals; up to 250 of these principals can be Google groups. Each occurrence of a principal counts towards these limits. For example, if the `bindings` grant 50 different roles to `user:alice@example.com`, and not to any other principal, then you can add another 1,450 principals to the `bindings` in the `Policy`.

fields.name: etag
fields.description: `etag` is used for optimistic concurrency control as a way to help prevent simultaneous updates of a policy from overwriting each other. It is strongly suggested that systems make use of the `etag` in the read-modify-write cycle to perform policy updates in order to avoid race conditions: An `etag` is returned in the response to `getIamPolicy`, and systems are expected to put that etag in the request to `setIamPolicy` to ensure that their change will be applied to the same version of the policy. **Important:** If you use IAM Conditions, you must include the `etag` field whenever you call `setIamPolicy`. If you omit this field, then IAM allows you to overwrite a version `3` policy with a version `1` policy, and all of the conditions in the version `3` policy are lost.

types.name: Binding
types.description: Associates `members`, or principals, with a `role`.

fields.name: role
fields.description: Role that is assigned to the list of `members`, or principals. For example, `roles/viewer`, `roles/editor`, or `roles/owner`. For an overview of the IAM roles and permissions, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/roles-overview). For a list of the available pre-defined roles, see [here](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/understanding-roles).

fields.name: members
fields.description: Specifies the principals requesting access for a Google Cloud resource. `members` can have the following values: * `allUsers`: A special identifier that represents anyone who is on the internet; with or without a Google account. * `allAuthenticatedUsers`: A special identifier that represents anyone who is authenticated with a Google account or a service account. Does not include identities that come from external identity providers (IdPs) through identity federation. * `user:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a specific Google account. For example, `alice@example.com` . * `serviceAccount:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a Google service account. For example, `my-other-app@appspot.gserviceaccount.com`. * `serviceAccount:{projectid}.svc.id.goog[{namespace}/{kubernetes-sa}]`: An identifier for a [Kubernetes service account](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/kubernetes-service-accounts). For example, `my-project.svc.id.goog[my-namespace/my-kubernetes-sa]`. * `group:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a Google group. For example, `admins@example.com`. * `domain:{domain}`: The G Suite domain (primary) that represents all the users of that domain. For example, `google.com` or `example.com`. * `principal://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/subject/{subject_attribute_value}`: A single identity in a workforce identity pool. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/group/{group_id}`: All workforce identities in a group. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/attribute.{attribute_name}/{attribute_value}`: All workforce identities with a specific attribute value. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/*`: All identities in a workforce identity pool. * `principal://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/subject/{subject_attribute_value}`: A single identity in a workload identity pool. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/group/{group_id}`: A workload identity pool group. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/attribute.{attribute_name}/{attribute_value}`: All identities in a workload identity pool with a certain attribute. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/*`: All identities in a workload identity pool. * `deleted:user:{emailid}?uid={uniqueid}`: An email address (plus unique identifier) representing a user that has been recently deleted. For example, `alice@example.com?uid=123456789012345678901`. If the user is recovered, this value reverts to `user:{emailid}` and the recovered user retains the role in the binding. * `deleted:serviceAccount:{emailid}?uid={uniqueid}`: An email address (plus unique identifier) representing a service account that has been recently deleted. For example, `my-other-app@appspot.gserviceaccount.com?uid=123456789012345678901`. If the service account is undeleted, this value reverts to `serviceAccount:{emailid}` and the undeleted service account retains the role in the binding. * `deleted:group:{emailid}?uid={uniqueid}`: An email address (plus unique identifier) representing a Google group that has been recently deleted. For example, `admins@example.com?uid=123456789012345678901`. If the group is recovered, this value reverts to `group:{emailid}` and the recovered group retains the role in the binding. * `deleted:principal://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/subject/{subject_attribute_value}`: Deleted single identity in a workforce identity pool. For example, `deleted:principal://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/my-pool-id/subject/my-subject-attribute-value`.

types.name: Expr
types.description: Represents a textual expression in the Common Expression Language (CEL) syntax. CEL is a C-like expression language. The syntax and semantics of CEL are documented at https://github.com/google/cel-spec. Example (Comparison): title: "Summary size limit" description: "Determines if a summary is less than 100 chars" expression: "document.summary.size() < 100" Example (Equality): title: "Requestor is owner" description: "Determines if requestor is the document owner" expression: "document.owner == request.auth.claims.email" Example (Logic): title: "Public documents" description: "Determine whether the document should be publicly visible" expression: "document.type != 'private' && document.type != 'internal'" Example (Data Manipulation): title: "Notification string" description: "Create a notification string with a timestamp." expression: "'New message received at ' + string(document.create_time)" The exact variables and functions that may be referenced within an expression are determined by the service that evaluates it. See the service documentation for additional information.

fields.name: expression
fields.description: Textual representation of an expression in Common Expression Language syntax.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Optional. Title for the expression, i.e. a short string describing its purpose. This can be used e.g. in UIs which allow to enter the expression.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional. Description of the expression. This is a longer text which describes the expression, e.g. when hovered over it in a UI.

fields.name: location
fields.description: Optional. String indicating the location of the expression for error reporting, e.g. a file name and a position in the file.

types.name: Byte
types.description: The `Byte` scalar type represents byte value as a Buffer

types.name: SetIamPolicyRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for `SetIamPolicy` method.

types.name: Policy_Input
types.description: An Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy, which specifies access controls for Google Cloud resources. A `Policy` is a collection of `bindings`. A `binding` binds one or more `members`, or principals, to a single `role`. Principals can be user accounts, service accounts, Google groups, and domains (such as G Suite). A `role` is a named list of permissions; each `role` can be an IAM predefined role or a user-created custom role. For some types of Google Cloud resources, a `binding` can also specify a `condition`, which is a logical expression that allows access to a resource only if the expression evaluates to `true`. A condition can add constraints based on attributes of the request, the resource, or both. To learn which resources support conditions in their IAM policies, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/conditions/resource-policies). **JSON example:** ``` { "bindings": [ { "role": "roles/resourcemanager.organizationAdmin", "members": [ "user:mike@example.com", "group:admins@example.com", "domain:google.com", "serviceAccount:my-project-id@appspot.gserviceaccount.com" ] }, { "role": "roles/resourcemanager.organizationViewer", "members": [ "user:eve@example.com" ], "condition": { "title": "expirable access", "description": "Does not grant access after Sep 2020", "expression": "request.time < timestamp('2020-10-01T00:00:00.000Z')", } } ], "etag": "BwWWja0YfJA=", "version": 3 } ``` **YAML example:** ``` bindings: - members: - user:mike@example.com - group:admins@example.com - domain:google.com - serviceAccount:my-project-id@appspot.gserviceaccount.com role: roles/resourcemanager.organizationAdmin - members: - user:eve@example.com role: roles/resourcemanager.organizationViewer condition: title: expirable access description: Does not grant access after Sep 2020 expression: request.time < timestamp('2020-10-01T00:00:00.000Z') etag: BwWWja0YfJA= version: 3 ``` For a description of IAM and its features, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/).

inputFields.name: version
inputFields.description: Specifies the format of the policy. Valid values are `0`, `1`, and `3`. Requests that specify an invalid value are rejected. Any operation that affects conditional role bindings must specify version `3`. This requirement applies to the following operations: * Getting a policy that includes a conditional role binding * Adding a conditional role binding to a policy * Changing a conditional role binding in a policy * Removing any role binding, with or without a condition, from a policy that includes conditions **Important:** If you use IAM Conditions, you must include the `etag` field whenever you call `setIamPolicy`. If you omit this field, then IAM allows you to overwrite a version `3` policy with a version `1` policy, and all of the conditions in the version `3` policy are lost. If a policy does not include any conditions, operations on that policy may specify any valid version or leave the field unset. To learn which resources support conditions in their IAM policies, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/conditions/resource-policies).

inputFields.name: bindings
inputFields.description: Associates a list of `members`, or principals, with a `role`. Optionally, may specify a `condition` that determines how and when the `bindings` are applied. Each of the `bindings` must contain at least one principal. The `bindings` in a `Policy` can refer to up to 1,500 principals; up to 250 of these principals can be Google groups. Each occurrence of a principal counts towards these limits. For example, if the `bindings` grant 50 different roles to `user:alice@example.com`, and not to any other principal, then you can add another 1,450 principals to the `bindings` in the `Policy`.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: `etag` is used for optimistic concurrency control as a way to help prevent simultaneous updates of a policy from overwriting each other. It is strongly suggested that systems make use of the `etag` in the read-modify-write cycle to perform policy updates in order to avoid race conditions: An `etag` is returned in the response to `getIamPolicy`, and systems are expected to put that etag in the request to `setIamPolicy` to ensure that their change will be applied to the same version of the policy. **Important:** If you use IAM Conditions, you must include the `etag` field whenever you call `setIamPolicy`. If you omit this field, then IAM allows you to overwrite a version `3` policy with a version `1` policy, and all of the conditions in the version `3` policy are lost.

types.name: Binding_Input
types.description: Associates `members`, or principals, with a `role`.

inputFields.name: role
inputFields.description: Role that is assigned to the list of `members`, or principals. For example, `roles/viewer`, `roles/editor`, or `roles/owner`. For an overview of the IAM roles and permissions, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/roles-overview). For a list of the available pre-defined roles, see [here](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/understanding-roles).

inputFields.name: members
inputFields.description: Specifies the principals requesting access for a Google Cloud resource. `members` can have the following values: * `allUsers`: A special identifier that represents anyone who is on the internet; with or without a Google account. * `allAuthenticatedUsers`: A special identifier that represents anyone who is authenticated with a Google account or a service account. Does not include identities that come from external identity providers (IdPs) through identity federation. * `user:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a specific Google account. For example, `alice@example.com` . * `serviceAccount:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a Google service account. For example, `my-other-app@appspot.gserviceaccount.com`. * `serviceAccount:{projectid}.svc.id.goog[{namespace}/{kubernetes-sa}]`: An identifier for a [Kubernetes service account](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/kubernetes-service-accounts). For example, `my-project.svc.id.goog[my-namespace/my-kubernetes-sa]`. * `group:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a Google group. For example, `admins@example.com`. * `domain:{domain}`: The G Suite domain (primary) that represents all the users of that domain. For example, `google.com` or `example.com`. * `principal://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/subject/{subject_attribute_value}`: A single identity in a workforce identity pool. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/group/{group_id}`: All workforce identities in a group. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/attribute.{attribute_name}/{attribute_value}`: All workforce identities with a specific attribute value. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/*`: All identities in a workforce identity pool. * `principal://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/subject/{subject_attribute_value}`: A single identity in a workload identity pool. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/group/{group_id}`: A workload identity pool group. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/attribute.{attribute_name}/{attribute_value}`: All identities in a workload identity pool with a certain attribute. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/*`: All identities in a workload identity pool. * `deleted:user:{emailid}?uid={uniqueid}`: An email address (plus unique identifier) representing a user that has been recently deleted. For example, `alice@example.com?uid=123456789012345678901`. If the user is recovered, this value reverts to `user:{emailid}` and the recovered user retains the role in the binding. * `deleted:serviceAccount:{emailid}?uid={uniqueid}`: An email address (plus unique identifier) representing a service account that has been recently deleted. For example, `my-other-app@appspot.gserviceaccount.com?uid=123456789012345678901`. If the service account is undeleted, this value reverts to `serviceAccount:{emailid}` and the undeleted service account retains the role in the binding. * `deleted:group:{emailid}?uid={uniqueid}`: An email address (plus unique identifier) representing a Google group that has been recently deleted. For example, `admins@example.com?uid=123456789012345678901`. If the group is recovered, this value reverts to `group:{emailid}` and the recovered group retains the role in the binding. * `deleted:principal://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/subject/{subject_attribute_value}`: Deleted single identity in a workforce identity pool. For example, `deleted:principal://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/my-pool-id/subject/my-subject-attribute-value`.

types.name: Expr_Input
types.description: Represents a textual expression in the Common Expression Language (CEL) syntax. CEL is a C-like expression language. The syntax and semantics of CEL are documented at https://github.com/google/cel-spec. Example (Comparison): title: "Summary size limit" description: "Determines if a summary is less than 100 chars" expression: "document.summary.size() < 100" Example (Equality): title: "Requestor is owner" description: "Determines if requestor is the document owner" expression: "document.owner == request.auth.claims.email" Example (Logic): title: "Public documents" description: "Determine whether the document should be publicly visible" expression: "document.type != 'private' && document.type != 'internal'" Example (Data Manipulation): title: "Notification string" description: "Create a notification string with a timestamp." expression: "'New message received at ' + string(document.create_time)" The exact variables and functions that may be referenced within an expression are determined by the service that evaluates it. See the service documentation for additional information.

inputFields.name: expression
inputFields.description: Textual representation of an expression in Common Expression Language syntax.

inputFields.name: title
inputFields.description: Optional. Title for the expression, i.e. a short string describing its purpose. This can be used e.g. in UIs which allow to enter the expression.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: Optional. Description of the expression. This is a longer text which describes the expression, e.g. when hovered over it in a UI.

inputFields.name: location
inputFields.description: Optional. String indicating the location of the expression for error reporting, e.g. a file name and a position in the file.

types.name: GetIamPolicyRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for `GetIamPolicy` method.

types.name: GetPolicyOptions_Input
types.description: Encapsulates settings provided to GetIamPolicy.

inputFields.name: requestedPolicyVersion
inputFields.description: Optional. The maximum policy version that will be used to format the policy. Valid values are 0, 1, and 3. Requests specifying an invalid value will be rejected. Requests for policies with any conditional role bindings must specify version 3. Policies with no conditional role bindings may specify any valid value or leave the field unset. The policy in the response might use the policy version that you specified, or it might use a lower policy version. For example, if you specify version 3, but the policy has no conditional role bindings, the response uses version 1. To learn which resources support conditions in their IAM policies, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/conditions/resource-policies).

types.name: TestIamPermissionsResponse
types.description: Response message for `TestIamPermissions` method.

fields.name: permissions
fields.description: A subset of `TestPermissionsRequest.permissions` that the caller is allowed.

types.name: TestIamPermissionsRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for `TestIamPermissions` method.

inputFields.name: permissions
inputFields.description: The set of permissions to check for the `resource`. Permissions with wildcards (such as `*` or `storage.*`) are not allowed. For more information see [IAM Overview](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/overview#permissions).

types.name: IapSettings_Input
types.description: The IAP configurable settings.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Required. The resource name of the IAP protected resource.

types.name: AccessSettings_Input
types.description: Access related settings for IAP protected apps.

inputFields.name: identitySources
inputFields.description: Optional. Identity sources that IAP can use to authenticate the end user. Only one identity source can be configured.

types.name: GcipSettings_Input
types.description: Allows customers to configure tenant IDs for a Cloud Identity Platform (GCIP) instance for each application.

inputFields.name: tenantIds
inputFields.description: Optional. GCIP tenant IDs that are linked to the IAP resource. `tenant_ids` could be a string beginning with a number character to indicate authenticating with GCIP tenant flow, or in the format of `_` to indicate authenticating with GCIP agent flow. If agent flow is used, `tenant_ids` should only contain one single element, while for tenant flow, `tenant_ids` can contain multiple elements.

inputFields.name: loginPageUri
inputFields.description: Login page URI associated with the GCIP tenants. Typically, all resources within the same project share the same login page, though it could be overridden at the sub resource level.

types.name: CorsSettings_Input
types.description: Allows customers to configure HTTP request paths that'll allow HTTP `OPTIONS` call to bypass authentication and authorization.

inputFields.name: allowHttpOptions
inputFields.description: Configuration to allow HTTP `OPTIONS` calls to skip authentication and authorization. If undefined, IAP will not apply any special logic to `OPTIONS` requests.

types.name: OAuthSettings_Input
types.description: Configuration for OAuth login&consent flow behavior as well as for OAuth Credentials.

inputFields.name: loginHint
inputFields.description: Domain hint to send as hd=? parameter in OAuth request flow. Enables redirect to primary IDP by skipping Google's login screen. https://developers.google.com/identity/protocols/OpenIDConnect#hd-param Note: IAP does not verify that the id token's hd claim matches this value since access behavior is managed by IAM policies.

inputFields.name: clientId
inputFields.description: Optional. OAuth 2.0 client ID used in the OAuth flow. This allows for client sharing. The risks of client sharing are outlined here: https://cloud.google.com/iap/docs/sharing-oauth-clients#risks.

inputFields.name: clientSecret
inputFields.description: Optional. Input only. OAuth secret paired with client ID.

inputFields.name: clientSecretSha256
inputFields.description: Output only. OAuth secret SHA256 paired with client ID.

inputFields.name: programmaticClients
inputFields.description: Optional. List of client ids allowed to use IAP programmatically.

types.name: PolicyDelegationSettings_Input
types.description: PolicyDelegationConfig allows google-internal teams to use IAP for apps hosted in a tenant project. Using these settings, the app can delegate permission check to happen against the linked customer project. This is only ever supposed to be used by google internal teams, hence the restriction on the proto.

inputFields.name: iamServiceName
inputFields.description: The DNS name of the service (e.g. "resourcemanager.googleapis.com"). This should be the domain name part of the full resource names (see https://aip.dev/122#full-resource-names), which is usually the same as IamServiceSpec.service of the service where the resource type is defined.

inputFields.name: iamPermission
inputFields.description: Permission to check in IAM.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The **relative** name of the resource, which is the URI path of the resource without the leading "/". See https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/conditions-resource-attributes#resource-name for examples used by other GCP Services. This field is **required** for services integrated with resource-attribute-based IAM conditions and/or CustomOrgPolicy. This field requires special handling for parents-only permissions such as `create` and `list`. See the document linked below for further details. See go/iam-conditions-sig-g3#populate-resource-attributes for specific details on populating this field.

inputFields.name: type
inputFields.description: The public resource type name of the resource. It is configured using the official_name of the ResourceType as defined in service configurations under //configs/cloud/resourcetypes. For example, the official_name for GCP projects is set as 'cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com/Project' according to //configs/cloud/resourcetypes/google/cloud/resourcemanager/prod.yaml This field is **required** for services integrated with resource-attribute-based IAM conditions and/or CustomOrgPolicy. This field requires special handling for parents-only permissions such as `create` and `list`. See the document linked below for further details. See go/iam-conditions-sig-g3#populate-resource-attributes for specific details on populating this field.

inputFields.name: service
inputFields.description: The name of the service this resource belongs to. It is configured using the official_service_name of the Service as defined in service configurations under //configs/cloud/resourcetypes. For example, the official_service_name of cloud resource manager service is set as 'cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com' according to //configs/cloud/resourcetypes/google/cloud/resourcemanager/prod.yaml This field is **required** for services integrated with resource-attribute-based IAM conditions and/or CustomOrgPolicy. This field requires special handling for parents-only permissions such as `create` and `list`. See the document linked below for further details. See go/iam-conditions-sig-g3#populate-resource-attributes for specific details on populating this field.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: The service defined labels of the resource on which the conditions will be evaluated. The semantics - including the key names - are vague to IAM. If the effective condition has a reference to a `resource.labels[foo]` construct, IAM consults with this map to retrieve the values associated with `foo` key for Conditions evaluation. If the provided key is not found in the labels map, the condition would evaluate to false. This field is in limited use. If your intended use case is not expected to express resource.labels attribute in IAM Conditions, leave this field empty. Before planning on using this attribute please: * Read go/iam-conditions-labels-comm and ensure your service can meet the data availability and management requirements. * Talk to iam-conditions-eng@ about your use case.

inputFields.name: expectedNextState
inputFields.description: The proto or JSON formatted expected next state of the resource, wrapped in a google.protobuf.Any proto, against which the policy rules are evaluated. Services not integrated with custom org policy can omit this field. Services integrated with custom org policy must populate this field for all requests where the API call changes the state of the resource. Custom org policy backend uses these attributes to enforce custom org policies. For create operations, GCP service is expected to pass resource from customer request as is. For update/patch operations, GCP service is expected to compute the next state with the patch provided by the user. See go/federated-custom-org-policy-integration-guide for additional details.

inputFields.name: locations
inputFields.description: The locations of the resource. This field is used to determine whether the request is compliant with Trust Boundaries. Usage: - Must not be empty for services in-scope for Trust Boundaries. Once Trust Boundaries is GA, empty values will cause the request to be rejected if customers enforce Trust Boundaries on the parent CRM nodes. - For global resources: use a single value of "global". - For regional/multi-regional resources: use name of the GCP region(s) where the resource exists (e.g., ["us-east1", "us-west1"]). For multi-regional resources specify the name of each GCP region in the resource's multi-region. NOTE: Only GCP cloud region names are supported - go/cloud-region-names.

types.name: NextStateOfTags_Input
types.description: Used for calculating the next state of tags on the resource being passed for the CheckCustomConstraints RPC call. The detail evaluation of each field is described in go/op-create-update-time-tags and go/tags-in-orgpolicy-requests.

inputFields.name: tags
inputFields.description: If TagsFullState is initialized, the values in this field fully represent all the tags in the next state (the current tag values are not used). If tags.size() == 0, the next state of tags would be no tags for evaluation purposes. Only one type of tags reference (numeric or namespace) is required to be passed.

inputFields.name: tagsToUpsert
inputFields.description: Tags that’ll be updated or added to the current state of tags for evaluation purposes. If a key exists in both "tags_to_upsert" and "tag_keys_to_remove", the one in "tag_keys_to_remove" is ignored. Only one type of tags reference (numeric or namespace) is required to be passed.

inputFields.name: tagKeysToRemove
inputFields.description: Keys of the tags that should be removed for evaluation purposes. IMPORTANT: Currently only numeric references are supported. Once support for namespace references is added, both the tag references (numeric and namespace) will be removed.

inputFields.name: tags
inputFields.description: If TagsFullStateForChildResource is initialized, the values in this field represent all the tags in the next state for the child resource. Only one type of tags reference (numeric or namespace) is required to be passed. IMPORTANT: This field should only be used when the target resource IAM policy name is UNKNOWN and the resource's parent IAM policy name is being passed in the request.

types.name: PolicyName_Input
types.description: An internal name for an IAM policy, based on the resource to which the policy applies. Not to be confused with a resource's external full resource name. For more information on this distinction, see go/iam-full-resource-names.

inputFields.name: type
inputFields.description: Resource type. Types are defined in IAM's .service files. Valid values for type might be 'storage_buckets', 'compute_instances', 'resourcemanager_customers', 'billing_accounts', etc.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: Identifies an instance of the type. ID format varies by type. The ID format is defined in the IAM .service file that defines the type, either in path_mapping or in a comment.

inputFields.name: region
inputFields.description: For Cloud IAM: The location of the Policy. Must be empty or "global" for Policies owned by global IAM. Must name a region from prodspec/cloud-iam-cloudspec for Regional IAM Policies, see go/iam-faq#where-is-iam-currently-deployed. For Local IAM: This field should be set to "local".

types.name: ReauthSettings_Input
types.description: Configuration for IAP reauthentication policies.

inputFields.name: maxAge
inputFields.description: Optional. Reauth session lifetime, how long before a user has to reauthenticate again.

types.name: AllowedDomainsSettings_Input
types.description: Configuration for IAP allowed domains. Lets you to restrict access to an app and allow access to only the domains that you list.

inputFields.name: enable
inputFields.description: Optional. Configuration for customers to opt in for the feature.

inputFields.name: domains
inputFields.description: Optional. List of trusted domains.

types.name: WorkforceIdentitySettings_Input
types.description: WorkforceIdentitySettings allows customers to configure workforce pools and OAuth 2.0 settings to gate their applications using a third-party IdP with access control.

inputFields.name: workforcePools
inputFields.description: The workforce pool resources. Only one workforce pool is accepted.

types.name: OAuth2_Input
types.description: The OAuth 2.0 Settings

inputFields.name: clientId
inputFields.description: The OAuth 2.0 client ID registered in the workforce identity federation OAuth 2.0 Server.

inputFields.name: clientSecret
inputFields.description: Input only. The OAuth 2.0 client secret created while registering the client ID.

inputFields.name: clientSecretSha256
inputFields.description: Output only. SHA256 hash value for the client secret. This field is returned by IAP when the settings are retrieved.

types.name: ApplicationSettings_Input
types.description: Wrapper over application specific settings for IAP.

inputFields.name: cookieDomain
inputFields.description: The Domain value to set for cookies generated by IAP. This value is not validated by the API, but will be ignored at runtime if invalid.

types.name: CsmSettings_Input
types.description: Configuration for RCToken generated for service mesh workloads protected by IAP. RCToken are IAP generated JWTs that can be verified at the application. The RCToken is primarily used for service mesh deployments, and can be scoped to a single mesh by configuring the audience field accordingly.

inputFields.name: rctokenAud
inputFields.description: Audience claim set in the generated RCToken. This value is not validated by IAP.

types.name: AccessDeniedPageSettings_Input
types.description: Custom content configuration for access denied page. IAP allows customers to define a custom URI to use as the error page when access is denied to users. If IAP prevents access to this page, the default IAP error page will be displayed instead.

inputFields.name: accessDeniedPageUri
inputFields.description: The URI to be redirected to when access is denied.

inputFields.name: generateTroubleshootingUri
inputFields.description: Whether to generate a troubleshooting URL on access denied events to this application.

inputFields.name: remediationTokenGenerationEnabled
inputFields.description: Whether to generate remediation token on access denied events to this application.

types.name: AttributePropagationSettings_Input
types.description: Configuration for propagating attributes to applications protected by IAP.

inputFields.name: expression
inputFields.description: Optional. Raw string CEL expression. Must return a list of attributes. A maximum of 45 attributes can be selected. Expressions can select different attribute types from `attributes`: `attributes.saml_attributes`, `attributes.iap_attributes`. The following functions are supported: - filter `.filter(, )`: Returns a subset of `` where `` is true for every item. - in ` in `: Returns true if `` contains ``. - selectByName `.selectByName()`: Returns the attribute in `` with the given `` name, otherwise returns empty. - emitAs `.emitAs()`: Sets the `` name field to the given `` for propagation in selected output credentials. - strict `.strict()`: Ignores the `x-goog-iap-attr-` prefix for the provided `` when propagating with the `HEADER` output credential, such as request headers. - append `.append()` OR `.append()`: Appends the provided `` or `` to the end of ``. Example expression: `attributes.saml_attributes.filter(x, x.name in ['test']).append(attributes.iap_attributes.selectByName('exact').emitAs('custom').strict())`

inputFields.name: outputCredentials
inputFields.description: Optional. Which output credentials attributes selected by the CEL expression should be propagated in. All attributes will be fully duplicated in each selected output credential.

inputFields.name: enable
inputFields.description: Optional. Whether the provided attribute propagation settings should be evaluated on user requests. If set to true, attributes returned from the expression will be propagated in the set output credentials.

types.name: TunnelDestGroup_Input
types.description: A TunnelDestGroup.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Identifier. Identifier for the TunnelDestGroup. Must be unique within the project and contain only lower case letters (a-z) and dashes (-).

inputFields.name: cidrs
inputFields.description: Optional. Unordered list. List of CIDRs that this group applies to.

inputFields.name: fqdns
inputFields.description: Optional. Unordered list. List of FQDNs that this group applies to.

types.name: Brand_Input
types.description: OAuth brand data. NOTE: Only contains a portion of the data that describes a brand.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Output only. Identifier of the brand. NOTE: GCP project number achieves the same brand identification purpose as only one brand per project can be created.

inputFields.name: supportEmail
inputFields.description: Support email displayed on the OAuth consent screen.

inputFields.name: applicationTitle
inputFields.description: Application name displayed on OAuth consent screen.

inputFields.name: orgInternalOnly
inputFields.description: Output only. Whether the brand is only intended for usage inside the G Suite organization only.

types.name: IdentityAwareProxyClient_Input
types.description: Contains the data that describes an Identity Aware Proxy owned client.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Output only. Unique identifier of the OAuth client.

inputFields.name: secret
inputFields.description: Output only. Client secret of the OAuth client.

inputFields.name: displayName
inputFields.description: Human-friendly name given to the OAuth client.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
